title: AMC Entertainment Q4 Earnings Beat Expectations
text:
AMC Entertainment delivered strong fourth-quarter results that exceeded Wall Street forecasts. The theater chain reported revenue of $1.3 billion, up more than 18% year-over-year, driven by blockbuster releases including Gladiator II, Wicked, Moana 2, and Mufasa: The Lion King.

FINANCIAL PERFORMANCE
Adjusted EBITDA more than tripled to $164.8 million from $49.9 million in the prior year. The company generated over $200 million in operating cash flow and approximately $114 million in free cash flow—the strongest quarterly cash generation since the pandemic.

Net losses narrowed significantly to $136 million from $182 million year-over-year. The stock initially declined 3% before rallying nearly 7% following the announcement, closing at $3.50.

OPERATIONAL HIGHLIGHTS
AMC welcomed over 62 million patrons globally during Q4, marking a post-pandemic fourth-quarter attendance record and representing a 20% increase versus 2023. Per-patron food and beverage revenue reached $7.15, an all-time Q4 record.

The company reduced debt by $375 million throughout 2024 and ended the year with $630 million in available cash reserves.

CEO OUTLOOK
CEO Adam Aron stated that these results "underscore our continued progress" toward recovery. He expressed optimism about "what we believe will be material growth in the industry-wide box office" during 2025 and beyond, highlighting planned expansions of premium and large-format screens as part of the company's "Go Plan."
